title: COPY
description: Copies the value of a key to a new key.
complexity: O(N) worst case for collections, where N is the number of nested items. O(1) for string values.

redis_py.signature: copy(source: str, destination: str, destination_db: Optional[int] = None, replace: bool = False)

returns.description: 1 if source was copied. 0 if source was not copied.

returns.description: 1 if source was copied. 0 if source was not copied.

redis_rb.signature: copy(source, destination, db: nil, replace: false)

content:
This command copies the value stored at the `source` key to the `destination` key. By default, the `destination` key is created in the logical database used by the connection. The `DB` option allows specifying an alternative logical database index for the destination key. The command returns zero when the `destination` key already exists. The `REPLACE` option removes the `destination` key before copying the value to it. ## Examples ``` SET dolly "sheep" COPY dolly clone GET clone ``` ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active\* | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active\* | For Active-Active or clustered databases, the source and destination keys must be in the same hash slot.

\*Not supported for stream consumer group info. | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="copy-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if _source_ was copied. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _source_ was not copied. -tab-sep- One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if _source_ was copied. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _source_ was not copied. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SET dolly "sheep"
COPY dolly clone
GET clone
intro:
This command copies the value stored at the `source` key to the `destination`
key.

By default, the `destination` key is created in the logical database used by the
connection. The `DB` option allows specifying an alternative logical database
index for the destination key.

The command returns zero when the `destination` key already exists. The
`REPLACE` option removes the `destination` key before copying the value to it.
notes: This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
This command copies the value stored at the `source` key to the `destination`
key.

By default, the `destination` key is created in the logical database used by the
connection. The `DB` option allows specifying an alternative logical database
index for the destination key.

The command returns zero when the `destination` key already exists. The
`REPLACE` option removes the `destination` key before copying the value to it.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
```
SET dolly "sheep"
COPY dolly clone
GET clone
```

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active\* | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active\* | For Active-Active or clustered databases, the source and destination keys must be in the same hash slot.

\*Not supported for stream consumer group info. |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="copy-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if _source_ was copied.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _source_ was not copied.

-tab-sep-

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if _source_ was copied.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if _source_ was not copied.

{{< /multitabs >}}
